title: Formally Verified Patent Analysis via Dependent Type Theory: Machine-Checkable Certificates from a Hybrid AI + Lean 4 Pipeline
summary: arXiv:2604.18882v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present a formally verified framework for patent analysis as a hybrid AI + Lean 4 pipeline. The DAG-coverage core (Algorithm 1b) is fully machine-verified once bounded match scores are fixed.
content: arXiv:2604.18882v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present a formally verified framework for patent analysis as a hybrid AI + Lean 4 pipeline. The DAG-coverage core (Algorithm 1b) is fully machine-verified once bounded match scores are fixed.

title: Easy Samples Are All You Need: Self-Evolving LLMs via Data-Efficient Reinforcement Learning
summary: arXiv:2604.18639v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Previous LLMs-based RL studies typically follow either supervised learning with high annotation costs, or unsupervised paradigms using voting or entropy-based rewards.
content: arXiv:2604.18639v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Previous LLMs-based RL studies typically follow either supervised learning with high annotation costs, or unsupervised paradigms using voting or entropy-based rewards.

title: FASE : A Fairness-Aware Spatiotemporal Event Graph Framework for Predictive Policing
summary: arXiv:2604.18644v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Predictive policing systems that allocate patrol resources based solely on predicted crime risk can unintentionally amplify racial disparities through feedback driven data bias.
content: arXiv:2604.18644v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Predictive policing systems that allocate patrol resources based solely on predicted crime risk can unintentionally amplify racial disparities through feedback driven data bias.

title: Curiosity-Critic: Cumulative Prediction Error Improvement as a Tractable Intrinsic Reward for World Model Training
summary: arXiv:2604.18701v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Local prediction-error-based curiosity rewards focus on the current transition without considering the world model's cumulative prediction error across all visited transitions.
content: arXiv:2604.18701v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Local prediction-error-based curiosity rewards focus on the current transition without considering the world model's cumulative prediction error across all visited transitions.
